title: How to Prompt Google Jules for Research
description: We ran 500+ Google Jules sessions across 9 domains and built a fully automated content factory.

schemas.headline: How to Prompt Google Jules for Research — 24 Lessons from 500+ Sessions
schemas.description: Complete guide to prompting Google Jules for web scraping, original research, and automated content factories across multiple domains.

mainEntity.name: Can Google Jules make HTTP requests and scrape websites?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Jules runs in a GitHub Codespace with full internet access. It can make HTTPS GET/HEAD requests, parse HTML, follow redirects, and save results to JSON — but it cannot render JavaScript or bypass anti-scraping measures.

mainEntity.name: How many Jules sessions can you run per day?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Jules allows 300 sessions per day per account with up to 60 concurrent sessions. Each session takes roughly 20-45 minutes to complete.

mainEntity.name: How do you prevent Jules from generating fake data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Include explicit anti-hallucination phrases like 'null over fake data, always' and define validation ranges for every numeric field. Jules follows these constraints when clearly stated.

mainEntity.name: What is the best way to automate Jules at scale?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use a dispatch cron to send sessions via the Jules API, a review cron to merge PRs, and a post-merge validation script to catch missing elements like GA tags, nav bars, and sitemaps.

mainEntity.name: How do you fix zero-click searches from Jules-generated content?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rewrite meta descriptions to create curiosity gaps — tease the data without revealing the key stat. Instead of stating the answer, make users click to find it.
